patches.summary: Auto-resolve site_uuid from BENTO_SITE_UUID env when --site-uuid flag not passed.
patches.reason: Bento requires site_uuid on every API call; flag-only was poor UX for env-driven setups.

patches.summary: Doctor reachability probe hits /api/v1/stats/site (real API) instead of / (Cloudflare-walled marketing site).
patches.reason: Direct probes of / were misclassified as 'API blocked by Cloudflare' interstitial; the real API is reachable just fine.

patches.summary: Wire bento resources (tags/fields/broadcasts/subscribers) into syncResourcePath() + auto-inject site_uuid query param.
patches.reason: Generator emitted only 'experimental' as a sync target; subscribers gracefully degrade to a sync_warning on 422 Enterprise gate.

patches.summary: parseSinceDuration now accepts absolute ISO dates (2026-05-19) in addition to relative durations (7d, 24h, 1w, 30m).
patches.reason: Snapshot diff and cohort FTS were rejecting commonly-used absolute date inputs.
Dogfood was using the fake UUID from examples and hitting 401 Access denied on the real API.

patches.summary: Two new novel commands for populating local subscriber store without Enterprise tier: subscribers fetch-batch (--emails-from file, one-by-one /fetch/subscribers?email=X), subscribers import-csv (--from bento-export.csv).
patches.reason: Bento's /fetch/search bulk endpoint is Enterprise-only; without these, the 4 analytical novel commands (churn-risk, winback, pre-delete, find) had no data source.

patches.summary: emptyLocalStoreOK helper + file-not-found guards in events/fields/hygiene/sync_diff/tags/broadcasts commands for verify-friendly behavior.
patches.reason: Phase 5 dogfood runs commands without test fixtures; commands now emit valid JSON envelopes under --json instead of erroring.

patches.summary: research.json narrative quickstart updated to use real command paths (auth status, sync --resources tags,fields,broadcasts) instead of placeholders; README + SKILL regenerated.
patches.reason: validate-narrative was failing on placeholder examples (--pk/--sk/--site-uuid flags that don't exist).

patches.summary: auth set-token now accepts/persists all three Bento credentials (publishable key + secret key + site UUID) as positional args; SaveCredential signature widened from (token) to (pk, sk, siteUuid); auth setup + auth status prose corrected.
patches.reason: Greptile P1: SaveCredential() wrote only BentoPublishableKey, but AuthHeader() requires BOTH pk AND sk (Bento Basic auth) plus site_uuid on every request. Single-arg set-token left users with 401s the moment env vars were unset.

patches.summary: sync.go injects site_uuid via resolveSiteUUID("", flags) instead of os.Getenv("BENTO_SITE_UUID") so the config-file path is honoured even without the env var set; syncResource() signature gained *rootFlags param (call sites in sync.go + channel_workflow.go updated).
patches.reason: Greptile P1: every other command resolved site_uuid via the (flag -> env -> config) helper; sync skipped step 3 and silently failed for users who had only run auth set-token without exporting BENTO_SITE_UUID.

patches.summary: ftsRowID swapped from 31-multiplier polynomial hash to fnv64a (stdlib hash/fnv) with 0x00 delimiter between scope and id.
patches.reason: Greptile P2: Bento IDs are short and numerically similar ("1464", "1465", "70064351"). The polynomial hash has weak avalanche on these inputs; a collision on the truncated 63-bit rowid would cause DELETE WHERE rowid=? to silently nuke the wrong FTS row, leaving stale/missing search results. fnv64a is order-sensitive on a flat byte stream and has much stronger distribution; the delimiter prevents scope/id boundary aliasing.
